BigBoss Commerce  ·  Operator Guide  ·  How it works · Purchasing → Inventory → Ledger

Procure-to-Pay

A purchase order is not a status field — it is the trigger for two independent resolutions that must stay in lockstep. This guide follows one PO end to end and shows what actually happens underneath: how receiving resolves into INVENTORY (a Flow move, a quant increment, a cost layer) AND into the LEDGER (a GR/IR accrual), and how posting the bill clears that accrual into Accounts Payable. Understand this once and every downstream report — stock valuation, GR/IR, A/P aging — makes sense.

11 steps Branch Head Office (HO-001) callout = where to click EN  ·  translation-ready

Flow — inventory-purchase-orders

  1. 01 Open Purchase Orders
  2. →
  3. 02 Raise the order
  4. →
  5. 03 Terms decide the accounting path
  6. →
  7. 04 Create + approve
  8. →
  9. 05 Receiving resolves INVENTORY
  10. →
  11. 06 The same receipt resolves the LEDGER
  12. →
  13. 07 Reopen the PO to bill it
  14. →
  15. 08 3-way match — the over-billing guard
  16. →
  17. 09 The bill CLEARS GR/IR into Accounts Payable
  18. →
  19. 10 GR/IR report — the reconciliation surface
  20. →
  21. 11 Payment settles A/P
1

STEP 01·NAVIGATE

Open Purchase Orders

Purchase Orders is where a buying INTENT is recorded. Creating a PO touches nothing physical and nothing financial yet — no stock moves, no journal posts. It only becomes real when goods are received. That separation is the whole point: intent, then resolution.

2

STEP 02·CLICK

Raise the order

New Purchase Order opens a side sheet: supplier, terms, and the lines you're buying. At this moment the PO is a Draft — a document, not a transaction. Approve it and it's authorised to receive.

3

STEP 03·SELECT

Terms decide the accounting path

This one field forks the whole resolution. CASH: receipt settles immediately (Dr Inventory / Cr Cash), no payable. CREDIT: receipt accrues against a GR/IR clearing account and a separate vendor bill later becomes the payable. Pick Credit to see the full two-step — the pattern every ERP-grade purchase follows.

4

STEP 04·CLICK

Create + approve

Add lines by barcode or SKU, then Create Purchase Order. Approval moves it to 'approved' — the goods can now be received. Still zero ledger impact: you have committed to buy, not received or owed anything.

5

STEP 05·RESOLVE

Receiving resolves INVENTORY

When you Receive, the Flow WMS kernel does three things atomically — this is the inventory resolution. (1) It creates a MOVE from the supplier's virtual vendor location into your stock location. (2) It increments the SKU's QUANT — the on-hand number Stock Levels shows. (3) It opens a COST LAYER at the received unit cost, which is what later values COGS (FIFO/AVCO) when you sell. A receipt is never a flag flip; it is these three records.

6

STEP 06·RESOLVE

The same receipt resolves the LEDGER

Simultaneously, the purchase-received bridge posts the accounting — here on the Journal Entries list. It debits Inventory (1164) and credits GR/IR (2125), 'Goods Received / Invoice Received'. Note what it does NOT do: it does not credit Accounts Payable, because no vendor invoice exists yet. GR/IR is the placeholder that says 'we hold the goods, we owe someone, we just haven't seen the paper.'

7

STEP 07·CLICK

Reopen the PO to bill it

Open the received PO's detail. Its action bar is pinned to the bottom of the sheet, so Receive, Post Vendor Bill and Record Payment stay one click away no matter how long the line-items run — the primary actions are never buried below the fold.

8

STEP 08·TYPE

3-way match — the over-billing guard

Post Vendor Bill opens the match. The field pre-fills with the PO total; you overwrite it with the amount on the vendor's real paper invoice. The system reconciles three numbers — what was billed, what the PO agreed, what was received. Within tolerance it posts silently; beyond tolerance it warns or blocks (configurable), and stamps the variance on the journal entry. This is the control that stops paying a supplier more than you agreed or received.

9

STEP 09·RESOLVE

The bill CLEARS GR/IR into Accounts Payable

Posting the bill books the second half of the two-step: it DEBITS GR/IR (2125) — cancelling the credit the receipt made — debits claimable Input VAT (1701), and credits Accounts Payable (2111), net of any VDS withheld (Cr 2136). The GR/IR account nets to zero: received value in, billed value out. You now hold a real, aging-tracked payable and reclaimable input VAT — and inventory was only ever debited once, at receipt.

10

STEP 10·RESOLVE

GR/IR report — the reconciliation surface

Any PO received but not yet billed leaves a non-zero GR/IR (2125) balance. This report lists exactly those — 'goods we hold but haven't been invoiced for' — grouped by PO with aging. Finance clears them before period close; the close gate blocks the period if GR/IR is not zero, guaranteeing inventory and payables can never silently drift apart across periods.

11

STEP 11·CLICK

Payment settles A/P

Record Payment is the final resolution: Dr Accounts Payable (2111) / Cr Cash or Bank, matched to the bill so the payable clears. Until then the amount sits on A/P Aging with its due date. Cash → goods → GR/IR → A/P → paid: five states, each an explicit ledger entry, all traceable back to this one purchase order.
